Join The Attachment Code
The Attachment Code
Fearful Avoidant Journal
Understanding Your Pattern

You are not broken. You are not toxic. You are someone whose nervous system learned that love and pain live in the same house. This is a private space to begin understanding that — with honesty, with compassion, and without the story that you are too much or not enough.

What this journal is

A private space to understand the fearful avoidant pattern — not to fix yourself, but to finally see yourself clearly. Every entry lives only on your device.

Speak or write

Every response field has a microphone button. Tap it to speak your answer — your words transcribe in real time. Speaking often reveals more than writing. Both are valid.

Private by design

Your entries are saved locally on this device only. No server. No database. No one but you can access what you write here.

Move the sliders to check in with which portals are most active today.
Choose Your Portal
The Spiral
Rumination · Catastrophising
When the mind takes silence and turns it into catastrophe. The story that builds before anything has actually happened.
The Run
Withdrawal · Disappearing
The sudden withdrawal. Disappearing before they can leave. The exit that happens before you have decided to take it.
The Good Moment
Fear of Joy · Self-Sabotage
The fear inside the good moment. The self-sabotage that arrives when things are going well. Waiting for joy to collapse.
The Push & Pull
Oscillation · Ambivalence
Wanting them, then needing space. Needing space, then wanting them. The oscillation that exhausts everyone including you.
The Original Wound
Origin · Early Learning
Where the pattern was first learned. The earliest relationship that taught you love was both necessary and dangerous.
The Return
Repair · Coming Back
Coming back after the run. Repair. The part that wants to try again.

Daily Practice

Seven steps. Work through them in order, or drop into wherever feels most true today.

1
Arrival
Before your mind — check in with your body. Where are you on the spectrum right now? Pulled toward connection or pulled toward distance?
ContractedActivatedNumbRestlessOpenTenderGuardedReachingSafeUnsettled
2
The Story
About someone you love. About yourself. About what's about to happen. What narrative is running in the background right now?
3
The Evidence
Not the feeling — the verifiable fact. What has actually happened, without interpretation? What is the evidence for it — and what is the evidence against it?
4
The Origin
Where have you felt this before? How far back does this particular fear go? Where did you first learn to tell yourself this story?
5
The Need
Not what it's demanding. Not what it's doing. What does it actually need — underneath the behaviour?
6
The Choice
One small, conscious choice about how you respond — instead of reacting from the pattern. Not a grand gesture. Just one thing.
7
The Closing
Not performance. Not toxic positivity. One honest thing — however small — that you're willing to see about yourself today.

Private by design. Your entries are saved locally on this device only. No one else can access them.

Guided Prompts

Tell the guide what you're working through. She'll create personalised prompts, practices, or exercises written specifically for where you are right now — in this relationship, in this pattern, in this moment.

Understanding what triggered me
Examining whether my thoughts are real or assumed
Working through the urge to run
Understanding a pattern I keep repeating
Processing shame after a difficult episode
Moving toward repair
Connecting current patterns to earlier experiences
Writing to someone I've hurt or lost

this week

The pattern that showed up most

What was most activated for you this week? The spiral, the run, the push and pull — what was loudest?

Something that shifted

What insight, however small, changed something? A moment you caught the spiral early, a moment you stayed instead of running.

The moment I'm most proud of

One moment — however small — where you chose differently. Where the pattern didn't completely run the show.

This Week's Actions
Complete your daily 7-step spread
Catch the spiral before it completes — write it down instead of acting on it
Stay in one difficult conversation instead of withdrawing from it
Let one good thing be good without questioning it
Attempt one act of repair — however small
Speak one honest need out loud to someone safe
Closing Ritual

You are not trying to become someone who never feels the pull. You are becoming someone who feels it — and chooses anyway. Every small act of staying, of returning, of speaking the truth — that is the work. That is the whole work.
